twitter_thread: Salesforce is spending $3.6 billion on Fin. That's $1.2 million per employee.
Fin's AI platform handles 30,000 customer inquiries per hour, a 50% increase for Salesforce
Salesforce must integrate Fin's tech within 6 months to avoid disrupting 100,000+ workflows
75% of customers still prefer human support, a challenge for AI-driven services
Fin's acquisition will spark 5-7 similar M&A deals in the AI customer service space within a year
Salesforce's move signals a 20% increase in AI adoption across the industry
How will you balance human touch with AI in your customer service strategy? #aicustomerservice #salesforce
pack.linkedin: Salesforce's $3.6 billion acquisition of Fin highlights a 30% increase in AI-driven customer inquiries. To succeed, Salesforce must integrate Fin's technology within 6 months, merging it with their existing 100,000+ workflows. With 75% of customers still preferring human support, companies must weigh the benefits of AI against the need for human interaction. As AI adoption is expected to rise by 20% across the industry, what's the most critical factor for companies to consider when implementing AI in their customer service strategies?
